title: HTTP/1.1 Message Framing and Length Precedence · Language Lens: Framing Completeness Evidence
summary: A recipient reads until EOF for every body, trusts duplicate Content-Length values, accepts Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length, and makes a framing decision before status and method semantics are known. Planned artifact: Add and verify the framing completeness evidence increment in RFC 9112 message-body length decision table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Record framing source, expected bytes, observed bytes, completion, residual buffer, close requirement, and rejection reason.
Parse decimal Content-Length values without overflow and accept repeated or list values only when every valid value is identical.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Framing Completeness Evidence · Theory Framing Completeness Evidence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 8877 in a canary edge service advertising HTTP/3 with bounded fallback. Build deterministic Go evidence to apply framing precedence and close on Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length ambiguity; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then append a second pipelined message and prove the first parser leaves the exact residual bytes. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Record framing source, expected bytes, observed bytes, completion, residual buffer, close requirement, and rejection reason.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Framing Completeness Evidence contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Framing Completeness Evidence · Theory Framing Completeness Evidence.
